title: Asynchronous Architecture Decisions for Go Services · Language Lens: Request Reply Boundary
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must make coupling, buffering, fan-out, replay, and failure costs observable at Go service boundaries; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the request reply boundary increment in a Go service coupling and broker-fit decision record. Learners progress through pred…
objectives: Evaluate request/reply over messaging against ordinary HTTP using correlation, timeout, cancellation, failure ambiguity, and capacity evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Distinguish competing consumers on one queue from publish/subscribe using one queue per subscriber responsibility; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
